topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating monotony, mirroring the dull ache settling in my chest. I stared at the spreadsheet, the numbers swimming before my eyes, tracing the discrepancy with a shaky finger. Sarah, the junior analyst, had just presented her quarterly projections, and the figures she cited for her bonus structure seemed astronomical compared to my own modest compensation. A tight knot formed just beneath my sternum. I forced a smile, a brittle thing that didn't reach my eyes, and nodded along as the meeting concluded. Later, at my desk, I couldn't focus on the reports. Every time I glanced at my pay stub, a cold wave washed over me, leaving a clammy feeling on my palms.

[story 2]

"Wow, Sarah, those numbers are incredible," Mark said, leaning back in his chair with an air of casual triumph. I managed a weak chuckle, the sound feeling thin and reedy in the quiet cubicle farm. Internally, a distinct tightening began in my jaw. I watched Sarah gather her papers, her posture radiating a quiet confidence I desperately lacked. The silence between us stretched, thick with unspoken comparisons. When the conversation finally moved on, I found myself meticulously organizing my desk supplies, a frantic need for order masking a swirling sense of inadequacy. My shoulders felt heavy, weighted down by an invisible burden.

[story 3]

I spent the afternoon meticulously re-reading the performance reviews, highlighting every metric where I felt I had fallen short. The reality hit me with a dull thud: Sarah’s compensation package was substantially larger than mine, a fact that settled into my gut like a stone. I picked up my lukewarm coffee, the bitter taste suddenly overwhelming. I began tapping my pen against the desk rhythmically, a nervous habit I couldn't seem to break. Looking out the window at the grey cityscape, I felt a profound sense of stagnation, as if the very air in the room had become too heavy to draw into my lungs.
